_forceAbilitySpawnDoc: TEST OVERRIDE (dev seam, like unlockAllCosmetics). When non-null, EVERY ability spawn (ability pads via spawnNewAbility, the brutal Ability round, and direct ability-tile pickups) yields this ability id instead of a random one — so a brand-new ability can be playtested immediately. Ships as null (normal random spawns); force-disabled in production (utils.js nulls it when NODE_ENV=production); FORCE_ABILITY_SPAWN env var overrides it per-run on a local server (e.g. FORCE_ABILITY_SPAWN=110 npm start).

aiRacers._traits: skill 0..1 (speed+precision+reaction); aggression 0..1 (punch); tempo 0..1 (spend vs hoard abilities); risk 0..1 (tight lava lines / corner-cut); focus race|combat|human|chaos; tilt rage = reckless when behind

playlists.name: Default
playlists.desc: Mostly featured, with the odd wildcard

gameModes.name: Team Race
gameModes.desc: Crimson vs Jade, shared team score

gameModes.name: Brutal Teams
gameModes.desc: Crimson vs Jade, every round brutal

lockedDoor._doc: Locked-door + key objective. Maps place N doors and N keys in the editor (1:1). At map-init the game randomly pairs each door with a key and assigns each pair a SHAPE, so the matching is different every round. A door's home cell is stamped to tileMap.door.id (a no-go wall). A key sits on the ground at its authored spot; the first eligible racer to touch it CARRIES it (like a held ability — shown as its shape around the kart, abilities/punch unaffected). Carrying the matching shape into its door UNLOCKS it (cell flips to normal/passable) for everyone. On death/infection/finish the carrier DROPS the key on nearby walkable ground; during collapse a loose key sitting on lava is CONSUMED (gone for the round — the door stays shut). Zombies can't carry keys.

water._doc: Deep water. Passive drive is almost nil (low acel + high drag) — you barely move unless you PUNCH to swim: a punch on water adds swimImpulse velocity in your held movement direction (scaled up a little by charge), so good swimmers average a speed between slow and normal. Swimming is just a punch, so it costs stamina and still knocks others back (the high drag dampens both ends). A killstreak fire shield (onFire>0) lets you STRIDE across water instead of swimming: the shield rides down on water exactly like it does on lava (steam rises while it burns) and only when it finally expires do you drop into the swim — so fire is a real water-crossing tool. Leaving water leaves you 'dripping': drive cut to dripMoveFactor for dripMs. A water edge that borders lava is a solid stone wall (engine bounce, like an empty-cell rim) — water is otherwise fully walkable. During a collapse water does NOT flash straight to lava: it 'slow-boils' for collapseBoilMs (a tiered simmer->boil->rolling warning) before converting, giving racers a few extra seconds to scramble off. canBeRandomed:false so the 'random' tile never rolls water for v1.

door._doc: A LOCKED DOOR barrier (see config.lockedDoor). Maps place doors + matching keys via the editor (1:1). At map-init the game converts each door's home cell to this id, which the engine treats as a no-go wall (bounceOffLockedDoors) blocking ALL players until the matching shaped key is carried into it. On unlock the cell flips back to normal (passable). canBeRandomed:false; never painted directly — only stamped at runtime.

orbitalBeam._doc: Aimed strike 'from orbit'. The origin + 8-way direction are LOCKED at cast (the holder's position/facing the moment they fire); the beam telegraphs along that fixed line for fuse ms, then strikes. On strike it MELTS ice it crosses to water, BURNS sand (the slow tile) it crosses to lava, and sets any kart standing in the line on fire like lava (lava-style death; honors invuln / Star Power / zombie immunity). The CASTER is NOT exempt — linger in your own beam and it burns you too (a self-burn is uncredited). beamLength/beamWidth size the rectangle (world is 1366x768, so the default spans the map); fuse is the 5s warn-up; aimerLength is the held-ability direction nub.

antlion.title: Antlions
antlion._doc: Sand is dangerous: linger on it and antlions erupt nearby and give chase. Thumpers pound the ground and keep antlions out of their radius (sanctuaries). Mutually exclusive with heatwave (it deletes the sand habitat).

boons._doc: Boons are the helpful counterpart to hazards: map-authorable placeables that AID a player. They share the hazard-kind registry (server/entities/boons.js registers them with helpful:true), live in the same runtime hazardList, and ride the same wire/drawer/editor pipeline — the helpful flag is what gates the three places hazard behavior diverges (lightning speed-up, AI repulsion, AI cell-penalty) and what the map classifier skips so a boon never reads as added difficulty. Ids start at 950 to stay clear of hazards (900s).

rechargeSpring._doc: Drive-over pit stop: instantly tops the punch-stamina bar back to full, clears the exhausted/overcharge latch, and resets the punch cooldown so you're immediately attack-ready. The spring is a GLOBAL shared charge — the first needy racer to reach a ready spring consumes it, then it drains and refills over cooldownMs before it can recharge anyone again (a full racer driving over a ready one doesn't waste it). That refill progress rides the wire netState slot (0=just drained .. 100=ready) so the client draws the drained->fill-ring->ready-pulse telegraph. colorWater is the foam palette used by the on-water render variant (a bubbling spring) so the pad reads against blue water.

slipstream._doc: A wind-current corridor: while overlapping, a gentle constant push along the author-set axis carries you up to currentSpeed (well below dashArrows' launch cap). Purely axial, so being shoved/driven backwards through it means the current fights you and carries you forward again. Chain several to build a long tunnel. radius is the circular hitbox (Hazard/Circle collision, +playerBaseRadius); width/height are the drawn footprint and are sized to sit INSIDE that hitbox so what you see is what's active (no dead zone at the corridor ends). colorWater is the foam palette used by the on-water render variant (a river current with wavy streamlines) so it reads against blue water.

guardHalo._doc: A floating ring you drive over to pick up a ONE-HIT SHIELD: the shield is per-player state that absorbs the next incoming hit (punch / bumper / bumper-wall / rotor / bomb / ice shot / cut / puck) and then pops, with no knockback, attribution, or infection applied for that hit. One shield per player at a time — driving over a halo while already shielded is a no-op (a topped-up racer never wastes a ready halo). The halo itself is a GLOBAL shared charge: the first unshielded racer to reach a ready halo claims the shield, then the halo drains and re-arms over cooldownMs before it can grant again. That refill progress rides the wire netState slot (0=just claimed .. 100=ready) so the client draws the drained->fill-ring->ready-pulse telegraph (mirrors Recharge Spring). colorWater is the palette used by the on-water render variant so the ring reads against blue water.

secondWindTotem._doc: A respawn flag you drive over to ATTUNE. Once attuned, EVERY death that round respawns you at the flag instead of ending your run — repeatedly, for as long as the flag survives. The death plays as a beat: you freeze at the death spot for respawnDelayMs (the local view slow-pans the camera from the death spot to the flag), then you reappear at the flag with respawnInvulnMs of grace. The flag is 'consumed' the moment the collapsing lava reaches its tile (tracked tick-by-tick via the safe flag → netState 0); after that it revives no one. IMPORTANT (team modes): a flag respawn is NOT a death — no team-points penalty and no kill credit are charged. Those only apply on a REAL death: the flag was never activated, or it has been burned/consumed by the time the delay elapses. Excludes infection deaths (a bitten/zombified racer is not revived) and only fires during racing/collapsing. On respawn you keep your notch and your velocity is zeroed. Driving over a DIFFERENT flag re-anchors you to it. The flag draws in a neutral colour and only recolours to YOUR kart colour on your own screen when you touch it (client-only, so it reads as 'you activated this'). color is the neutral cloth; colorWater is the on-water palette.

barriers._doc: Author-placed solid obstacles from the editor's 2-point tool. A barrier is a {x1,y1,x2,y2,style} line segment players can't pass through but slide along — the SAME deflection as the water/lava stone seam (engine.js bounceOffBarriers). Unlike hazards/boons these are NOT entities: they live in the map's own top-level `barriers` array (sibling of `hazards`), are precomputed into a segment list for collision, and ride the newMap payload raw. `styles` is the list of allowed `style` keys (validation only); the look for each is drawn by the shared client/scripts/barrierArt.js (used by both the game and the editor), not config-driven. maxCount/maxLength bound abuse at the validation boundary.

punchMomentum._doc: Punch knockback scales with how fast you're moving TOWARD your aim. floor/ceil are the bonus multiplier at zero vs full charge; refFrac is the fraction of maxVelocity (along facing) that counts as full charge.

punchStamina._doc: Stamina is the punch/charge fuel. A tap costs punchCost; holding to charge drains up to fullChargeCost (a full charge empties the bar). Below punchCost you can't punch; once exhausted you must regen back to exhaustRecover before punching again (set = punchCost so you can tap again the moment you've recovered one tap's worth), and you move at exhaustedMoveFactor speed until then. max/punchCost = taps from a full bar.

landLunge._doc: Double-tap punch on SOLID GROUND to LUNGE — a short repositioning burst, the land cousin of the water swim stroke. Two punch presses within doubleTapMs trigger it directly (decoupled from the punch throw so it fires regardless of the punch cooldown). It first brakes your velocity (punchThrowBrake, the same slow every swing applies) then adds `impulse` along your held movement direction. By design it is NOT a fast way to travel: `impulse` is only a few times cruise speed (a hop of a kart-length or two to clear a hazard, get off sand, or dodge a burn), and the cost makes it a NET slowdown — it empties your ENTIRE stamina bar and pauses regen for recoverMs, so you're stuck at the exhausted move penalty (no drive top-speed, no re-lunge, no punch) for that whole window while a racer who DIDN'T lunge keeps cruising and pulls ahead. Gated: requires a held direction (no move keys = no lunge) and at least minStaminaFrac of a full bar to start. On water a punch already swims, so the lunge only applies off water.

punchCharge._doc: Hold-to-charge: holding punch for maxChargeMs reaches a full charge that multiplies the momentum-scaled knockback by maxChargeMult (final force = momentum bonus x charge). Charge is capped by the stamina you can afford. Overcharge: holding past overchargeAfterMs starts a danger phase whose dark-red meter fills over overchargeFillMs; if you don't release in time the charge is wasted and you're locked in the exhausted move-penalty for exhaustLockMs. chargedHitFrac = how full a charge counts as a 'fully charged' hit (drives the thwack SFX).

iceDrift._doc: Drifting: holding a punch charge while on ice blends the kart's ice physics toward normal-tile grip by `grip` (0 = pure ice, 1 = full normal traction) — you slow slightly and gain steering control. Distance covered while drift-charging banks toward the Smooth Operator medal, but ONLY if the drift stays 'clean': the released punch must not land on (or clash with) anyone, and burning up in lava voids the pending credit. resolveMs = how long after a charged punch is thrown before its pending drift credit is judged (must exceed the ~100ms punch linger so `landed`/`clashed` have settled). releaseBrakeEase = on-ice softening of the punch-release brake: releasing ON ICE keeps punchThrowBrake x this of your velocity (1.15 = 15% more glide than a solid-ground release; capped at keeping 100%) — the full-strength stop felt too harsh coming out of a drift. keepMomentumOnRelease = playtest experiment: releasing a drift-charge on ice skips the release brake entirely.

punchClash._doc: Two players who punch each other while facing each other clash. If their forces are within tieMargin it's a standoff: both flung back by THEIR OWN momentum (reflectKick * bonus). Otherwise the STRONGER punch wins — the weaker is cancelled and the stronger lands normally. range = max owner separation; facingDot = min dot of facing vs direction-to-rival.

momentumRamp._doc: Momentum ramp: top speed builds while you hold a steady heading and resets when you cut hard or stop. The drive force is scaled by floor..1 — a freshly-started (or just-turned) kart drives at `floor` of its normal acceleration (so it cruises at `floor` of its usual top speed) and climbs to full over `rampTime` seconds of committed movement. resetDot = the cos() of the heading change that resets the ramp (0.4 ≈ 66°, so small steering nudges keep your momentum but a hard turn/reverse dumps it). resetSpeedMin = the hard-turn reset only fires when you're already carrying this much speed; a near-stationary kart (just starting, maneuvering, or shoved into a wall) keeps building so it can get going instead of being pinned at the floor. Full ramp is identical to the old physics, so this only ever slows you down — commit to a line to go fast.
